text.title: django-base-model Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for a Django BaseModel pattern.

rule.statement: Every domain model inherits from `BaseModel` with: (1) AutoField `id` as primary key (integer, for fast joins/indexes), (2) `uid = UUIDField(unique=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)` exposed publicly, (3) `created_at`, (4) `updated_at`. The integer PK NEVER leaves the database.

rationale.source: Mike Allen — UUIDs as PKs are slow; Two Scoops of Django

rule.rationale: Integer PKs index ~25% smaller than UUIDs in PostgreSQL and produce ~2x faster joins on large tables. UUIDs at the API layer prevent enumeration attacks and make sharding/exports portable.

rule.statement: BaseModel sets `Meta.abstract = True`. Every concrete inheritor inherits the four fields without table inheritance overhead.
rule.rationale: Concrete table inheritance creates an implicit JOIN every read. Abstract inheritance copies the fields into the concrete table — zero runtime cost.

rule.statement: API URLs use `uid` (UUID), not `id`. `path('orders/<uuid:uid>/', ...)`. Lookup helpers (`get_object_or_404(Order, uid=...)`).
rule.rationale: Exposing integer PKs leaks scale information and enables enumeration. UUID URLs are stable across DB exports and shards.

rule.statement: Every `ForeignKey` declares `on_delete=` explicitly. Default to `PROTECT` for parent-of-record relations; `CASCADE` only when child rows are semantically owned by the parent; `SET_NULL` for soft references.
rule.rationale: Implicit on_delete is a Django anti-pattern fixed in 2.0+. The default deletion semantics are part of the schema, not an implementation detail.

rule.statement: Composite query patterns get `Meta.indexes = [models.Index(fields=[...])]`. Single-column unique lookups use `unique=True`. Do NOT rely on PostgreSQL auto-creating indexes from FK alone — that catches only the FK column.
rule.rationale: Composite query patterns (`filter(user=u, status='active', created_at__gte=...)`) need a covering index. Django will not create one automatically.
